subject: DevOps
title: Shift Left
description: A fast breakdown of Shift Left for builders.

hook.text: Testing used to happen at the end. Security review came even later. Problems surfaced way too late.

buildup.text: Shift left means moving quality checks earlier in the pipeline — closer to the developer's commit.

discovery.text: Run linters at code time. Run unit tests before merge. Scan for vulnerabilities before deploy.

twist.text: The cost of a bug found in development is 100x cheaper than one found in production.

climax.text: Shift left applies to security, testing, compliance, and even documentation. Move it all earlier.

punchline.text: Find problems early, fix them cheap. Shift left.

quiz.question: What does 'shift left' mean in DevOps?
options: Moving quality checks earlier in the development pipeline
Deploying code to the left data center first
Assigning tasks to the left side of the Kanban board
